SEO Insertion on Does It Make Sense to Surrender an Endowment Policy? - Article Page
Keyword density and no. of target keywords has been mentioned as comments in the beginning
Suggested additional anchor texts are marked as comments in Yellow. (If required)
Keywords: surrender an endowment plan, endowment policy, endowment plan
Body Content:
Essentially, an endowment plan is a life insurance plan which covers the life of the individual insured. However, such a policy aids the insured in saving amounts regularly, over a specific period. As a result, the insured can benefit from a lump sum amount when the policy matures, provided the insured survives the tenure of the policy. Therefore, this lump sum amount helps to meet a range of financial needs of the insured, be it funding retirement, a child’s further education, etc.
In case of the demise of the policyholder during the tenure of the plan, a sum assured is handed over to beneficiaries of the insured. Thus, a life insurance plan which helps the insured to save money with a lump sum benefit at maturity is called an endowment plan. Sometimes, individuals may want to surrender an endowment plan, but they should consider certain aspects of this action first.
What is the Surrender Value of an Endowment Plan?
As insurance plans go, endowment plans are very advantageous. You get a great opportunity to invest your funds wisely, and with hardly any risk. Besides this, you also get benefits and life cover which give your beneficiaries protection when you are not around to provide an income. This is why an endowment policy is a popular way to invest and allocate wealth.  However beneficial an endowment plan may be, you may still wish to surrender your policy, for reasons  known to you. To do this, all you have to do is to stop your premium payments. Once you do this, the policy ceases to offer you coverage, and the perks that come with it. Furthermore, you are able to avail a value after you surrender your policy.
If you, the holder of a policy, stops paying the value of the premium during the tenure of the policy, you can surrender the endowment plan automatically. When you do this, you will receive a surrender amount. You should note that there are two kinds of surrender values with an endowment plan. One is a guaranteed value, and the other is a special value. Both are computed separately, according to specific formulas involved. The final and total amount that is specified by a surrender value is dependent on various factors. These include the amount of the premium, the paid premiums, bonuses incurred and other factors.
Should You Surrender Your Endowment Plan?
Most financial experts will advise you to refrain from surrendering an endowment plan. This is mainly due to the reasons mentioned below:
When you surrender your plan, you instantly lose any and all the cover that was previously provided by your plan. You are free to purchase another plan, but until you do, you are unprotected till the approval of your new plan comes through.
If you do opt for a new insurance plan, whether it is a new endowment policy or some other insurance plan, you may have to pay more. This is due to you being older while applying for the new plan or developing certain health conditions and other risks.
You may incur some loss in value in terms of the premiums you had paid for your endowment plan. You do get a surrender value, but this is typically not the total amount of your premiums paid. The loss incurred by you is based on the policy you have and the insurer you have chosen.
What is Paid Up Value?
For some reason, you may not be able to continue to pay your premium on your old endowment policy. As a result, you may decide to surrender your plan. However, before you do this, you should consider continuing with your plan at a reduced premium value. This is a possibility with many insurers and plans, and the reduced rate may be suitable enough for you to continue with your policy.
Conclusion
If you wish to surrender an endowment plan, you can do so easily. However, before you embark on a step like this, you should consider certain factors and make a decision. It is suggested that you should surrender an endowment plan only if you stand to gain a significant amount, rather than lose too much. Moreover, before you opt to buy a new plan, you must think carefully and choose one that gives you more benefits than your previous one. Apart from endowment plans, there are other products you may allocate your funds to, such as health insurance plans which give you cover for treatment costs.
